story.iWant: the agent to propose a high-level agenda with 4-8 sections after I provide my purpose and audience
story.soThat: I can structure my presentation around clear narrative stages before diving into details

tasks.task: Add step 2.5 to plan-deck/instructions.md (~line 145, after context collection) - Insert new step with goal "Generate and refine agenda structure" - Move before step 3 (narrative generation)
Implement agenda proposal logic - Analyze purpose, audience, key_points, desired_length - Generate 4-8 sections based on content analysis - Map each section to a narrative_role

Add AskUserQuestion tool call for section selection - Use multiSelect: true - Present sections in groups of 4 (tool limit) - Include section title as label, description as description - Store selections in agenda_sections variable
Handle custom section input via "Other" - If user provides custom text, parse into new section - Generate id, title, narrative_role, estimated_slides, description
Add validation for minimum sections - If fewer than 3 sections selected, display warning - Allow user to proceed anyway or select more

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given I have provided purpose, audience, key points, and length via the existing plan-deck prompts, When the agent analyzes my input, Then it proposes 4-8 agenda sections with: unique id (agenda-1, etc.), title, narrative_role (opening|context|problem|solution|evidence|cta), estimated_slides (1-3 per section), brief description (1 sentence)
And I can select/deselect sections via Claude Code's AskUserQuestion multi-select UI
And I can add custom sections via the "Other" option
And confirmed sections are stored in the workflow variable agenda_sections for Phase 2 discovery
And if I select fewer than 3 sections, I receive a warning that the presentation may be too brief

doc.title: Enhanced Planning Sessions - Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Primary context document containing brownfield codebase analysis, AskUserQuestion tool patterns, enhanced plan.yaml schema, and complete implementation guidance for the two-phase planning model.

doc.title: Epic 13: Enhanced Planning Sessions
doc.snippet: Epic defining the goal of transforming plan-deck from single-pass to two-phase planning with agenda structure generation and per-section deep discovery.

doc.title: Slide Builder Conventions
doc.snippet: Documents BMAD workflow patterns including instructions.md format, status.yaml state management, and template catalog system conventions.

doc.title: Today - Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Documents overall project architecture: Node.js runtime, vanilla HTML/CSS/JS frontend, BMAD workflow system, and file structure conventions.

artifact.reason: Primary modification target - insert new step 2.5 after context collection. Contains existing prompts for purpose, audience, key_points, desired_length that provide input for agenda generation.
Follows the new step 2.5 - will need to consume agenda_sections variable. Contains storyline generation and slide breakdown logic.
Add agenda_sections variable to track selected agenda items for Phase 2.
Reference for template matching - agenda sections eventually map to template use_cases during slide generation.

dependencies.note: No new dependencies required - uses existing Claude Code AskUserQuestion tool infrastructure

constraints.constraint: Workflow instructions use XML tags: step, action, check, ask, goto, output, template-output
AskUserQuestion tool allows maximum 4 questions per call, maximum 4 options per question
Each option needs "label" (short display text) and "description" (explanation)
multiSelect: true enables checkbox selection; false enables radio button single-select
User can always select "Other" for custom input - tool handles this automatically
Functions use camelCase, XML tags use kebab-case
Save plan.yaml after each modification (AC5.2.7 pattern)
Insert step 2.5 after line 144 in instructions.md, before step 3

interface.name: AskUserQuestion
Agenda Section Schema
interface.signature: { id: "agenda-N", title: "string", narrative_role: "opening|context|problem|solution|evidence|cta", estimated_slides: 1-3, description: "string (1 sentence)" }

interface.name: Workflow variables
interface.signature: {{purpose}}, {{audience}}, {{key_points}}, {{desired_length}} - inputs from step 2 {{agenda_sections}} - array output from step 2.5

tests.standards: No automated testing framework in place. Testing is manual via workflow execution. Run `/sb:plan-deck` in Claude Code and observe multi-select prompts and verify flow.

ideas.idea: Test agenda proposal with sample purposes: "Partnership pitch", "Product demo", "Quarterly review" - verify 4-8 sections generated with all required fields
Verify AskUserQuestion multi-select UI appears correctly with sections as options
Test "Other" custom input - enter custom section text, verify it's parsed into proper section structure
Verify agenda_sections variable contains all selected sections after confirmation
Test minimum sections warning - select only 2 sections, verify warning displayed about presentation being too brief
